verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is canonically a secreted serine protease without a transmembrane domain, showing cytoplasmic/secretory localization in baseline prostate epithelium. However, documented cell-surface expression in prostate cancer cell lines and patient-derived tumors has been confirmed by FACS, immunofluorescence, and confocal imaging. This cancer-specific surface display enables targeting by bispecific T-cell engagers (pasritamig), radioligand therapy, and CAR-T cells. The protein body reaches the outer leaflet exclusively in the transformed/oncogenic state, qualifying as cell-state-induced contextualization.
